original_hypothesis.title: Marketplace freeze locks all listed vesting tokens with no emergency unlist path
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Both listVesting and unlistVesting in the Marketplace have the isFreeze modifier, which requires !isMarketplaceFreeze. When the admin freezes the marketplace, ALL listed tokens become inaccessible — sellers cannot unlist to recover their vesting allocations, and the admin cannot perform emergency unlisting since the modifier applies to all callers. VestingManager.unlistVesting is only callable by the marketplace (onlyMarketplace), so there is no alternative recovery path during freeze.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The isFreeze modifier on unlistVesting creates a design flaw where emergency operations are blocked during the very state (freeze) when they are most needed. The VestingManager's unlistVesting function is exclusively gated by onlyMarketplace, leaving no admin bypass.

proof_of_signal.pattern: isFreeze modifier on unlistVesting prevents all callers (including admin) from unlisting during freeze; VestingManager.unlistVesting has onlyMarketplace with no admin bypass

preconditions: At least one listing is active (status == LIST with balance > 0)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: isFreeze modifier: require(!isMarketplaceFreeze) blocks unlistVesting for all callers during freeze
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin freeze (possibly in response to emergency) traps all listed tokens
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit; all listed vesting allocations locked for freeze duration

economic_questions: Can admin unfreeze, batch-unlist, and re-freeze atomically?
On a no-mempool chain, what is the risk window during brief unfreeze?
Could upgradeable marketplace be upgraded to add emergency unlist during freeze?
validation_requirements: Freeze marketplace, attempt unlist as seller and admin — confirm both revert
Verify no alternative path through VestingManager to recover tokens
Test unfreeze → unlist → re-freeze sequence and assess risk window

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No profit; value at risk = all listed vesting allocations

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Sellers cannot access listed tokens; time-sensitive vesting claims may be missed

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All sellers with active listings during freeze
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of listed allocation locked during freeze

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Trace isFreeze modifier on all marketplace functions
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm no bypass path through VestingManager during freeze

validation_result.impact_summary: When the marketplace is frozen, the isFreeze modifier on unlistVesting prevents all callers (including admin) from unlisting tokens. Listed vesting allocations are locked with no emergency recovery path during freeze.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies a design defect in the Marketplace contract. The `isFreeze` modifier is applied to `unlistVesting` (line ~151), which blocks ALL callers—including the admin—when `isMarketplaceFreeze` is true. While the admin has explicit authorization to call `unlistVesting` (line ~157: `listing.seller == msg.sender || msg.sender == IMarketplaceSetting(marketplaceSetting).s2Admin()`), the `isFreeze` modifier executes before the function body, preventing the admin from reaching the authorization check. The VestingManager's `unlistVesting` is gated by `onlyMarketplace`, providing no alternative recovery path. The admin's only workaround is to unfreeze the marketplace, perform unlisting, and re-freeze—but this opens a non-atomic window where all marketplace operations (listing, purchasing, etc.) become available during what is presumably an emergency state.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The isFreeze modifier at SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol:92-97 universally blocks all callers when frozen. The unlistVesting function at line ~151 applies this modifier. The admin authorization check at line ~157 is unreachable during freeze. VestingManager.unlistVesting at SecondSwap_VestingManager.sol has onlyMarketplace modifier with no admin bypass. No emergency unlist function exists anywhere in the codebase.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The modifier blocks ALL callers universally when marketplace is frozen, with no exception for admin emergency operations.
The isFreeze modifier runs before the function body, so the admin authorization check (s2Admin) at line ~157 is unreachable when the marketplace is frozen. Admin cannot perform emergency unlisting.

The onlyMarketplace modifier means only the Marketplace contract can call this function. There is no admin bypass, so VestingManager cannot be used as an alternative recovery path during marketplace freeze.
Admin can set/unset freeze, but unfreezing to perform emergency operations opens a window for all marketplace operations, defeating the freeze purpose.

calling_sequence: 1. Admin calls setMarketplaceStatus(true) on MarketplaceSetting to freeze marketplace
2. isMarketplaceFreeze is set to true in MarketplaceSetting storage
3. Admin (or seller) calls unlistVesting on Marketplace
5. require(!true) fails → transaction reverts with 'Marketplace is currently frozen'
6. Function body never executes, admin authorization check is never reached
7. VestingManager.unlistVesting is gated by onlyMarketplace, no direct admin call possible
8. Listed tokens remain locked in VestingManager for the duration of the freeze
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The isFreeze modifier at Marketplace.sol:92-97 calls IMarketplaceSetting(marketplaceSetting).isMarketplaceFreeze() which reads MarketplaceSetting.isMarketplaceFreeze (set to true by admin via setMarketplaceStatus). The require(!isMarketplaceFreeze) reverts, preventing execution of unlistVesting at line 151. The admin check at line 157 (msg.sender == s2Admin) is never evaluated. VestingManager.unlistVesting has onlyMarketplace at line 128, which requires msg.sender == marketplace. No admin can directly call it. No other function in the system can return listed vesting tokens to sellers.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts show standard marketplace deployment with freeze initially set to false. The defect exists in the contract code regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) Multiple sellers have active listings on the marketplace. (2) Admin detects an issue and freezes the marketplace via setMarketplaceStatus(true). (3) Sellers attempt to unlist their vestings to recover allocations — all calls revert with 'Marketplace is currently frozen'. (4) Admin attempts to unlist on behalf of sellers — also reverts at the isFreeze modifier before reaching the admin authorization check. (5) All listed vesting allocations remain locked in VestingManager for the entire freeze duration. The only workaround is for the admin to unfreeze (opening ALL operations), perform unlisting, then re-freeze — but this is non-atomic and exposes the marketplace to the very risk the freeze was meant to prevent.
validation_result.impact_assessment: All active marketplace listings are frozen during a marketplace freeze. Sellers cannot recover their vesting allocations. The admin cannot perform emergency unlisting despite having explicit authorization in the function body. Time-sensitive vesting claims may be missed if underlying tokens continue vesting during the freeze period. On chains with mempools, the unfreeze-unlist-refreeze workaround is vulnerable to frontrunning by other users who could list, purchase, or exploit other issues during the brief unfreeze window.
recommended_mitigations: Remove the isFreeze modifier from unlistVesting, or create a separate emergency unlist function without the freeze check that is restricted to admin only
Add an admin bypass within the isFreeze modifier: require(!isMarketplaceFreeze || msg.sender == s2Admin)
Add a dedicated emergencyUnlist function on VestingManager with onlyAdmin modifier that can return vesting tokens to sellers regardless of marketplace state
Consider implementing batch emergency operations so admin can unfreeze, process unlists atomically, and re-freeze in one transaction via a helper contract
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full system (Marketplace, MarketplaceSetting, VestingManager, StepVesting). 2. Create a vesting allocation for a seller. 3. Have seller call listVesting to create an active listing. 4. Admin calls setMarketplaceStatus(true) to freeze marketplace. 5. Seller calls unlistVesting — expect revert 'Marketplace is currently frozen'. 6. Admin calls unlistVesting — expect same revert (modifier blocks before admin check). 7. Attempt direct call to VestingManager.unlistVesting from admin — expect revert 'caller is not marketplace'. 8. Confirm no path exists to recover listed tokens during freeze.
uncertainties: Whether the admin would consider the unfreeze-unlist-refreeze workaround acceptable operational practice
Whether the Marketplace contract behind a proxy could be upgraded during freeze to add emergency unlist functionality (possible but requires careful proxy admin coordination)
The actual duration of marketplace freezes in production and the economic impact during that period
additional_testing_needed: Test the unfreeze-unlist-refreeze sequence on a chain with mempool to measure frontrunning risk window
Verify whether proxy upgrade during freeze state is possible and whether it would preserve listing state correctly
Test concurrent freeze/unfreeze operations to check for race conditions in multi-admin setups
